value.name: Inception Module
value.description: A building block consisting of parallel convolutional paths (1x1, 3x3, 5x5) and pooling operations, concatenated along the channel dimension, designed to extract features at multiple scales efficiently.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Introduced in GoogLeNet (Szegedy et al. 2014/2015), this paper refines it with factorized filters.

value.name: Factorized 5x5 Convolution
value.description: Replaces a single 5x5 convolution with two consecutive 3x3 convolutions. This reduces the parameter count and computational cost by exploiting translation invariance and weight sharing across the sliding window.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Novel application of decomposition to replace large square filters specifically within the Inception context to save compute.

value.name: Asymmetric Factorization
value.description: Factorizes n x n convolutions into a sequence of 1 x n and n x 1 convolutions. For example, a 3x3 convolution is replaced by a 1x7 followed by a 7x1 convolution on specific grid sizes to reduce computation by 33% relative to 3x3 or more for larger n.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Novel factorization strategy targeting rectangular receptive fields to optimize computational efficiency on intermediate feature maps.

value.name: Efficient Grid Size Reduction
value.description: A downsampling module that uses two parallel stride-2 branches: one pooling branch and one convolutional branch, whose outputs are concatenated. This avoids the representational bottleneck associated with standard pooling-only downsampling.
value.novelty_vs_prior: New module design to address the trade-off between computational cost and representational capacity during spatial reduction.

value.name: Label Smoothing Regularization (LSR)
value.description: A regularization technique that modifies the ground-truth label distribution from a one-hot vector to a mixture of the one-hot vector and a uniform distribution (or class prior), controlled by a smoothing parameter epsilon.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Proposed here as a mechanism to prevent over-confidence and improve generalization, interpreted as penalizing deviation from the label prior.

value.name: Batch Normalization
value.description: Normalizes the activations of each layer across the batch dimension to stabilize and accelerate training, reducing internal covariate shift.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Ioffe and Szegedy 2015. Used extensively here as a core component of the Inception modules and auxiliary classifiers.
